content:
Tshwane University of Technology (TUT) Entry Requirements 2026

Tshwane University of Technology (TUT) is one of South Africa's largest universities with campuses in Pretoria (Arcadia, Arts, Ga-Rankuwa, Soshanguve) and other regions. TUT offers a wide range of diplomas, degrees, and postgraduate qualifications across six faculties. This guide covers TUT's 2026 entry requirements for all faculties.

Check your eligibility using our free APS Calculator.

TUT Minimum APS Requirements by Faculty (2026)

FacultyMinimum APSKey Subject Requirements
Engineering & the Built Environment26Mathematics 4+, Physical Sciences 4+, English 3+
Information and Communication Technology (ICT)24Mathematics 4+, English 3+
Natural Sciences24Mathematics 4+, Physical Sciences or Life Sciences 4+, English 3+
Management Sciences22English 3+, Mathematics 3+ or Mathematical Literacy 4+
Humanities20English 3+, any other three subjects
Education22English 4+, subject specialisation requirements vary
Arts & Design20English 3+, portfolio may be required
Health Sciences22Life Sciences 4+, English 3+, varies by programme

Note: TUT calculates APS from your six best subjects excluding Life Orientation (or counting it at half value, max 2 points). Individual programme requirements may differ from the faculty minimums above — always check the specific programme on the TUT website.

How TUT Calculates APS

TUT uses the standard NSC APS calculation:

Percentage (%)APS Points
80–100%7
70–79%6
60–69%5
50–59%4
40–49%3
30–39%2
0–29%1

Life Orientation is counted at half value (maximum of 2 APS points) or excluded at TUT. Always use your six best academic subjects to calculate your APS for TUT.

TUT Popular Programme Requirements

Diploma in Civil Engineering

  • APS: 26+
  • Mathematics: Level 4 (50%+)
  • Physical Sciences: Level 4 (50%+)
  • English: Level 3 (40%+)

Bachelor of Technology / Advanced Diploma in Information Technology

  • APS: 24+
  • Mathematics: Level 4 (50%+)
  • English: Level 3 (40%+)

Diploma in Nursing

  • APS: 22+
  • Life Sciences: Level 4 (50%+)
  • English: Level 4 (50%+)
  • Mathematics or Mathematical Literacy: Level 3+ (40%+)

Diploma in Accounting

  • APS: 22+
  • Mathematics: Level 3 (40%+) or Mathematical Literacy Level 4 (50%+)
  • English: Level 3 (40%+)

Diploma in Education (BEd)

  • APS: 22+
  • English: Level 4 (50%+)
  • Subject specialisation requirements apply

TUT Application Process 2026

  1. Check requirements: Confirm you meet the APS and subject requirements for your chosen programme
  2. Apply online: Visit www.tut.ac.za and complete the online application form
  3. Application fee: R240 (non-refundable). Fee waivers available for applicants receiving social grants
  4. Documents required: Certified ID copy, Grade 11/12 results, proof of residence
  5. Application deadline: Typically September 30 for the following academic year

TUT Campuses

CampusLocationMain Faculties
Arcadia CampusPretoria (main campus)Engineering, ICT, Management, Humanities, Natural Sciences
Arts CampusPretoria CBDArts & Design
Ga-Rankuwa CampusGa-Rankuwa, PretoriaHealth Sciences, Science
Soshanguve North & SouthSoshanguve, PretoriaEngineering, Management Sciences
Emalahleni CampusEmalahleni, MpumalangaEngineering Technology
Polokwane CampusPolokwane, LimpopoManagement Sciences, ICT

NSFAS at TUT

TUT is a NSFAS-accredited institution. Students from households earning below R350,000 per year may qualify for NSFAS funding. Apply at www.nsfas.org.za from October each year for the following academic year.

Not quite meeting TUT's entry requirements? StudyBuddy's AI tutor can help you improve your Grade 11 or Grade 12 marks across all subjects — most students improve 15–25% within a few weeks. Start free today.
quickAnswer: TUT entry requirements vary by faculty: Engineering needs APS 26+, ICT 24+, Management Sciences 22+, Education 22+, and Humanities 20+. Life Orientation is counted at half value. Apply online at www.tut.ac.za before the September 30 deadline.

faqs.question: What is the minimum APS for TUT?
faqs.answer: TUT minimum APS requirements vary by faculty: Engineering & Built Environment requires 26+, ICT and Natural Sciences require 24+, Health Sciences, Education and Management Sciences require 22+, and Humanities requires 20+. Check specific programme requirements on the TUT website.

faqs.question: Does TUT accept Mathematical Literacy?
faqs.answer: Yes, some TUT programmes (especially in Management Sciences and Humanities) accept Mathematical Literacy, typically at Level 4 (50%+). However, Engineering, ICT, and Natural Sciences programmes require Pure Mathematics. Check your specific programme requirements.

faqs.question: How do I apply to TUT?
faqs.answer: Apply online at www.tut.ac.za. You'll need a certified copy of your ID, Grade 11 or Grade 12 results, proof of residence, and the application fee of R240. Applications typically close around September 30 for the following year.

faqs.question: Does TUT offer NSFAS?
faqs.answer: Yes, TUT is a NSFAS-accredited institution. Students from households earning below R350,000/year may qualify for NSFAS funding. Apply at www.nsfas.org.za from October each year.
